overview.brief_summary: An expert-level attacker compromises an employee's email account and uses it to gain access to the SCADA system, ultimately causing a denial-of-service attack on the wastewater treatment plant.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker sends a spear-phishing email to an employee with access to the transportation system's email account, tricking them into revealing their login credentials.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker uses the compromised email account to access the web-based interface for the SCADA system.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attacker uses the compromised email account to gain access to other systems within the organization, including the SCADA system's engineering workstation.

stage.name: Execution
stage.technique_name: T1094: Custom Command and Control Protocol
stage.description: The attacker uses the engineering workstation to upload malware that allows them to remotely access and control the SCADA system.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker uses the remote access to manipulate the SCADA system and cause a failure in the wastewater treatment plant's control system, resulting in the release of raw sewage into the environment.

Scenario.summary: A sophisticated attacker compromises an employee's email account and uses it to gain access to the SCADA system, leading to a denial-of-service attack on the wastewater treatment plant.
